Hey everybody, Tanner Sheldon here and I'm going to show you guys how to run a virtual group, workshop or mastermind just in a few easy steps just for those that are beginning and want to maybe tap into a virtual group before they go ahead and tackle an in-person group. So first things first, why a virtual group, why a virtual workshop and why a virtual mastermind? A few things. First thing, lower ticket prices for beginners. If you're not ready to drop in, you know, $5,000 for your students, $10,000, $25,000 all the way up to $250,000 like Dean and Tony do, this is a lower ticket price for your students. It's also easier to create and manage compared to an in-person one. It's easier for your students to commit to a weekend at home on the computer or ongoing help on a computer rather than, you know, spending three days away a couple of times a year. You also may be more comfortable for your first event to do it on camera rather than in front of a room. And the cost of the event will be much cheaper for the expert compared to if they do it in person when you got to spend a little bit more money. So all of those are great reasons is why a virtual would be a good step in the right direction for you. A workshop is where one person is kind of delivering their knowledge to the group. It's more hands on.
